items.title: Use dedicated security review automations so PR velocity can scale without trading away safety
items.why: Background security-review agents let teams keep review throughput high even as coding agents increase the amount of code landing each day.
how_to_steps: Create a PR-triggered security review automation with the repo hooks and communication channels it needs, such as GitHub plus Slack.
Route findings through a persistence or MCP layer that deduplicates semantically similar issues and emits a consistent report format.
Start in report-only mode, then enable PR comments, and only add a blocking gate after the automation proves it is finding real issues with acceptable precision.
Add follow-on automations for invariant drift and dependency patching so agents can re-check old code, run tests, and repair findings continuously.
items.evidence: Cursor says PR velocity increased 5x over nine months, and its Agentic Security Review ran on thousands of PRs and prevented hundreds of issues from reaching production.
items.try_today: Create one report-only security-review automation for active PRs, let it post to a private Slack channel for a week, and measure how many issues it catches before humans do.
